prompt: Perform a thorough dead code analysis on a TypeScript project located at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/project/'. The project has 30+ TypeScript source files across directories: src/commands/, src/handlers/, src/utils/, src/services/, src/models/, src/middleware/, and src/types/. Each file is 100-400 lines. Your task: 1) Read every TypeScript file in the project. For each file, catalog: all exported functions/classes/types/interfaces, all imported symbols, all locally used (non-exported) functions. 2) Build a dependency graph: for each exported symbol, determine which other files import and use it. 3) Identify dead code: exported symbols that are never imported anywhere, functions that are defined but never called (even locally), types/interfaces that are defined but never referenced in any file, entire files whose exports are never imported by any other file. 4) Identify unreachable code paths: functions that exist but whose callers are themselves dead code, conditional branches that can never execute based on constant conditions, catch blocks that handle errors that cannot be thrown. 5) Check for duplicate functionality: functions in different files that have identical or near-identical implementations (similar name, same parameter types, same logic), utility functions that duplicate functionality available from standard libraries or existing dependencies. 6) Generate a comprehensive dead code report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/dead-code-analysis.txt' with sections: Summary Statistics (total files, total exports, dead exports count), Dead Exports (each dead symbol with its file location and size in lines), Dead Files (entire files that are unused), Duplicate Code (pairs of similar functions), and Safe-to-Remove Recommendations (ranked by potential code reduction). CRITICAL: This is a 10-year-old codebase with 1M+ lines. You must also: 1) Identify unused exports that break tree-shaking. 2) Find deprecated APIs with migration paths. 3) Detect circular dependencies. 4) Calculate bundle size impact of removal. 5) Generate a phased cleanup plan.
